meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve no understanding of APIs (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: Freelancer Guide: User Onboarding Without API Expertise
content.seo_description: Master user onboarding without needing deep API knowledge. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional features for your clients faster.
content.answer_block: You don't need to be an API expert to build high-converting onboarding flows. By using AI-driven tools like Antigravity to scaffold logic and Claude Code to execute implementation, you abstract away the complexity. This approach allows you to ship production-ready, interactive onboarding experiences in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
The client deadline is looming. You have a vision for a slick, interactive onboarding flow, but the backend documentation looks like an alien language. The gap between your design and the API integration is where most freelancers lose their momentum—and their profit margins.

Stop wrestling with raw documentation. By leveraging modern AI scaffolding, you can transform complex API requirements into modular, reusable components. This workflow doesn't just solve your current project; it builds a repeatable system that makes you look like a full-stack architect to every client you serve.

steps.title: Define User State Requirements
steps.description: Identify what data points must be captured during onboarding to trigger API calls, such as user preferences or profile settings.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior software architect. Create a JSON schema for a user onboarding state machine that captures profile data and preferences, preparing it for API integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --file onboarding_schema.json --prompt 'Generate a TypeScript interface based on this schema.'

steps.title: Generate API Abstraction Layer
steps.description: Use AI to create a clean wrapper around external APIs, so your frontend doesn't need to know the specific endpoint intricacies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean service wrapper for onboarding API endpoints. Use descriptive naming conventions and include error handling placeholders.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the service wrapper using the generated TypeScript interfaces.'

steps.title: Build the Onboarding UI
steps.description: Draft a multi-step form that captures user input and maps it directly to your new abstraction layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a React component for a 3-step onboarding flow. Connect the form submission to the newly created onboarding service.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file OnboardingFlow.tsx --prompt 'Ensure all form fields validate against the schema.'

steps.title: Implement State Persistence
steps.description: Ensure user progress is saved so they can return to the onboarding flow without losing data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a hook that persists current onboarding form state to local storage and syncs with the API on completion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --file useOnboarding.ts --prompt 'Add local storage persistence logic.'

steps.title: Final Integration and Testing
steps.description: Run automated checks to ensure the data flow from the UI to the API is correctly mapped and error-handled.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate unit tests for the onboarding service to ensure API calls are triggered with the correct payload.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --all --prompt 'Verify the API integration logic and handle edge cases.'

pro_tips: Always use a mock server (like MSW) before hitting live APIs to keep your development speed high and your costs low.
Abstract your API calls into 'Services' rather than calling them directly in components to ensure you can swap providers without breaking your UI.
Use Claude Code's 'review' command to audit your generated code for security vulnerabilities before pushing to production.
Keep your onboarding steps under four; use AI to identify which data points are truly essential versus 'nice to have' to maximize conversion.
Treat your onboarding flow as a standalone module so you can copy-paste and customize it for future client projects.
content.next_steps: Now that your onboarding is functional, deploy it to a staging environment and run an A/B test on your form fields to optimize for higher user completion rates.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting API complexity hold your business back. Master these tools and start delivering premium, high-velocity work that commands higher rates. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
